title: A neighbor I'll never forget
summary: Describe one neighbor from your past and why you still remember them.
prompt: Write 7–10 sentences about one neighbor (from any city or country) who you still remember. Use past simple. Use linking adverbs like 'at first', 'later', 'however' to organise the story.
sampleText: When I was twelve, an old man named Mr. Suresh lived in the flat above us. He worked at a railway station for forty years before he retired. Every Sunday morning, he sat on the stairs with a small radio and listened to football news. At first, I thought he was strange because he wore the same brown jacket all year. Later, I learned he gave the money for new jackets to his sister, who was sick. After he moved away, I did not see him again. However, I still remember the sound of his radio, and the smell of the toast he sometimes shared with me.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: lived in the flat above us
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: sống trong căn hộ phía trên chúng tôi

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past simple for a complete story
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Tất cả các động từ chính (lived, worked, sat, wore, gave, moved) đều ở quá khứ đơn vì câu chuyện đã kết thúc.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: All the main verbs (lived, worked, sat, wore, gave, moved) are in the past simple because the story is finished.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Linking adverbs: at first / later / however
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'At first…', 'Later…', 'However…' giúp tổ chức câu chuyện theo thứ tự cảm xúc và thời gian.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'At first…', 'Later…', 'However…' help organise the story by emotion and time.

sampleTranslationVi: Khi tôi mười hai tuổi, một ông cụ tên là Mr. Suresh sống trong căn hộ phía trên chúng tôi. Ông đã làm việc ở một ga xe lửa trong bốn mươi năm trước khi nghỉ hưu. Mỗi sáng Chủ nhật, ông ngồi trên cầu thang với một chiếc radio nhỏ và nghe tin tức bóng đá. Lúc đầu, tôi nghĩ ông kỳ lạ vì ông mặc cùng một chiếc áo khoác màu nâu suốt cả năm. Sau này, tôi biết được rằng ông đã đưa tiền mua áo khoác mới cho người chị bị bệnh của mình. Sau khi ông chuyển đi, tôi không gặp lại ông nữa. Tuy nhiên, tôi vẫn nhớ âm thanh chiếc radio của ông, và mùi của miếng bánh mì nướng mà thỉnh thoảng ông chia cho tôi.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence is the most natural opening for a memory from your childhood?
options: When I am twelve, an old man lived above us.
When I was twelve, an old man lived in the flat above us.
When was I twelve, an old man living above.
When I will be twelve, an old man lived above us.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple in both clauses ('was', 'lived') fits a memory.
mcQuestions.hint: Both verbs should be in the past simple.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence that uses 'at first' / 'later' correctly.
options: At first, I thought he was strange. Later, I learned he gave the money to his sister.
At first I am thinking he strange. Later I learn he give money.
First at, I thought he strange. After later, I learned.
Later, I thought he strange. At first, I learned.
mcQuestions.explanation: Linking adverbs at the start of each sentence + past simple verbs is the natural pattern.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for 'At first' and 'Later' at the start of each sentence with past tense verbs.

options: However I did not see him again, I still remember his radio.
I did not see him again. However, I still remember the sound of his radio.
I did not see him however again the radio.
However however I still remember his radio.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'However' connects two ideas that contrast, with a comma after it at the start of a new sentence.
mcQuestions.hint: How does 'However' usually start a sentence in English?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence keeps the focus on a small, concrete memory?
options: I still remember the sound of his radio and the smell of his toast.
Old people are important in every culture and society.
Football news on the radio is very popular in Europe.
I have many neighbors and I like all of them very much.
mcQuestions.explanation: Only A is a small, concrete sensory memory of this one neighbor.
mcQuestions.hint: Which option mentions a specific sound or smell from the story?

criticalThinkingQuestion: Was there a person in your past you misunderstood at first? What changed your mind?
